title: Alcohol use mental health review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.
investigations.rationale: Supports alcohol screening purpose and primary-care context for alcohol use mental-health review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.

source.title: AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — Guidelines for Use in Primary Care, Second Edition

exact_section.heading: Purpose of the manual and context of alcohol screening

medication.text: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.
medication.rationale: Supports mood, function and contextual assessment for alcohol use mental-health review; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression

exact_section.heading: Sections 5–7 — depressive features and structured assessment

follow_up.text: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.
follow_up.rationale: Supports alcohol consumption, dependence and harm domains for alcohol use mental-health review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Alcohol pattern, last use, harms, dependence, withdrawal, AUDIT answers and score, mood, risk, diagnosis and clinician plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for alcohol use mental-health review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
